---
title: "Your Poster Title Goes Here — Make It Specific and Punchy"
authors:
  - name: "Naim U. Rashid, PhD"
    affiliations: "Department of Biostatistics, UNC Gillings School of Global Public Health"
  - name: "Co-author Name"
    affiliations: "Affiliation"
format:
  poster-typst:
    size: "48x36"
    poster-authors: "Naim U. Rashid, Co-author Name"
    departments: "UNC Gillings · UNC Lineberger Comprehensive Cancer Center"
    institution-logo: "RLlogoA.png"
    footer-text: "Rashid Lab · UNC Chapel Hill · 2026"
    keywords: "precision oncology, adaptive trials, biostatistics"
    num-columns: 3
    univ-logo-scale: 100
    title-font-size: "90pt"
    body-font-size: "26pt"
    sectionhead-font-size: "40pt"
    keywords-font-size: "22pt"
    authors-font-size: "32pt"
---

```{=typst}
// =============================================================================
// RASHID LAB — Poster Token Setup
// Mirrors the --rl-* tokens from naimurashid.github.io and lab-handbook/
// branding/colors.qmd. Update only the literals below if the brand shifts.
// =============================================================================

#let rl-navy          = rgb("#13294B")
#let rl-navy-deep     = rgb("#0B1D3A")
#let rl-carolina      = rgb("#4B9CD3")
#let rl-carolina-deep = rgb("#2E6A9F")
#let rl-graphite      = rgb("#5B6670")
#let rl-ink           = rgb("#1A202C")
#let rl-fog           = rgb("#F4F6F8")
#let rl-mist          = rgb("#E6EAEF")

// Typography defaults — Archivo for body, Source Serif 4 for the title,
// IBM Plex Mono for eyebrows/code/metadata.
#set text(font: "Archivo", fill: rl-ink, size: 26pt)
#show heading: set text(font: "Archivo", weight: "semibold", fill: rl-navy)
#show heading.where(level: 1): set text(font: "Source Serif 4", weight: "semibold", size: 90pt, fill: rl-navy)
#show heading.where(level: 2): set text(size: 40pt, fill: rl-navy)
#show heading.where(level: 3): set text(size: 30pt, fill: rl-navy)
#show raw: set text(font: "IBM Plex Mono", size: 0.95em)
#show link: set text(fill: rl-carolina-deep)
#show emph: set text(font: "Source Serif 4", style: "italic")

// Mono uppercase eyebrow helper
#let eyebrow(content) = text(
  font: "IBM Plex Mono",
  size: 22pt,
  weight: "medium",
  fill: rl-graphite,
  tracking: 0.05em,
  upper(content),
)
```

# Background {.unnumbered}

```{=typst}
#eyebrow[Motivation]
```

State the clinical or methodological problem in 2–3 sentences. Anchor it
in the population, scale, or constraint that makes the question worth
solving. End with a single-sentence summary of what your work does about
it.

# Methods

```{=typst}
#eyebrow[Approach]
```

- Bullet points are appropriate here when the methods decompose into
  distinct steps or components.
- Keep each bullet to one line if possible. Reviewers scan posters
  rather than read them.
- For mathematical content, render equations inline using `$\theta$`
  or as block displays.

The model fit a Bayesian hierarchical regression with semi-supervised
priors:

```{=typst}
#align(center)[
  $hat(theta) = arg max_(theta) sum_(i=1)^N log p(y_i mid(|) x_i, theta) + lambda || theta ||_2^2$
]
```

# Results

```{=typst}
#eyebrow[Key Finding]
```

State the single most important result first. Use a short sentence with
specific numbers (sample size, effect size, p-value, CI) rather than a
generic "we found significant differences."

::: {.center}
![Figure 1 caption goes here. Source Serif italic, mono "Figure N." prefix.](placeholder-figure.png){width="90%"}
:::

```{=typst}
#eyebrow[Secondary results]
```

Use a sub-eyebrow + content block to surface results that build on the
primary finding. Keep these tight; if it doesn't fit on the poster,
consider whether the audience needs it at all.

# Discussion

```{=typst}
#eyebrow[What this means]
```

Two short paragraphs at most:

1. Why the result matters in clinical/scientific terms.
2. What it changes about how the field should approach the problem.

Resist the urge to include limitations on a poster — readers infer
limitations from method choice, and posters are not the right venue
for caveat-heavy discussion. Save it for the paper.

# References & Acknowledgments

```{=typst}
#eyebrow[Cite & Thank]
```

1. Author A, Author B. *Journal Name* (Year).
2. Author C, Author D. *Journal Name* (Year).

Funding: NIH/NCI R01 CA000000, ARPA-H ADAPT, V Foundation Translational
Research Award. Co-PIs and lab members not listed as authors.

```{=typst}
#v(20pt)
#line(length: 100%, stroke: 1pt + rl-mist)
#v(10pt)
#text(size: 22pt, fill: rl-graphite, font: "IBM Plex Mono")[
  Contact: naim\@unc.edu · naimurashid.github.io
]
```
